title: AI Liquid Cooling Step-Function: Vera Rubin Forces 120-190 kW Racks
close_reason: Superseded by ai-liquid-cooling-mandate — same theme, same primary tickers, broader/more recent framing.
what_worked: Identified the Vera Rubin → liquid cooling step-function early; thesis remained directionally correct.
what_didnt: Filed too narrow a window (rubin-stepfunction); 3 days later we re-filed the same VRT+MOD thesis at a higher altitude (mandate). Two active opps with identical tickers exceeded the de-dup limit. Superseded close — entry/exit marked at current price; this opp did not run long enough to produce a true return.
would_have_done: Amend the existing opp instead of opening a parallel one when the angle is the same.

title: HBM Memory Sold Out Through 2026 — Pricing Power Returns to DRAM
close_reason: Superseded by dram-hbm-shortage — same theme, same primary tickers, broader/more recent framing.
what_worked: Caught the HBM oligopoly pricing power before the broader DRAM thesis crystallised.
what_didnt: Opened a MU-only version two days after the broader dram-hbm-shortage (MU + SNDK) opp; duplicate primary ticker and narrower mechanism. Superseded close — entry/exit marked at current price; this opp did not run long enough to produce a true return.
would_have_done: Roll the MU-only version into the broader dram-hbm-shortage opp.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock — Gulf Smelters Stranded by Hormuz Closure
close_reason: Superseded by aluminum-supply-shock — same theme, same primary tickers, broader/more recent framing.
what_worked: Identified the Hormuz angle correctly; AA continues to benefit from the supply gap.
what_didnt: Filed on the same day as the broader aluminum-supply-shock (AA + CENX) at lower conviction; redundant and weaker positioning. Superseded close — entry/exit marked at current price; this opp did not run long enough to produce a true return.
would_have_done: Skip the radar version when a High-Conviction version with the same primary ticker is filed simultaneously.

close_reason: Superseded by munitions-production-mobilization — same theme, same primary tickers, broader/more recent framing.

title: Data Center Copper Demand Supercycle
close_reason: Expired during 2026-06-19 housekeeping. 110 days old (well beyond 30-day On Our Radar deadline). Secular Trend pattern flagged WEAK in ledger (0/3 win rate) — would require 3 primary sources to promote to High Conviction, but existing sources are predominantly secondary press (Carbon Credits, MacroAnalytix, Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, HeyGoTrade). Thesis remains plausible — copper supercycle from AI/EV/grid demand is intact — but this specific articulation does not meet the promotion bar.
what_worked: Identified the underpriced copper demand story early; FCX is up since opening (entry price ~$61.05). Recognized that data center copper intensity (5-10x traditional) was an underrated angle.
what_didnt: Sourcing leaned too heavily on secondary publishers; primary documentation (FCX 10-K, SEC filings, ICSG bulletins, USGS Mineral Commodity Summary) was not directly cited. Pattern bias not respected.
would_have_done: Either close earlier when secular pattern weakness in ledger became apparent, or re-source from FCX SEC filings, ICSG quarterly bulletins, and CRU/Wood Mackenzie primary research to clear the WEAK-pattern bar.

title: Permian Oil Windfall — $100 Crude, Cheap Stocks, Record Cash Returns
close_reason: Expired during 2026-06-20 housekeeping. WTI now at $77.54 — below the thesis's own $80 monitoring threshold. Iran-US talks ongoing; Strait of Hormuz now described as 'completely open' at various points. The high-conviction case for sustained $100 oil windfall has materially weakened. Cash flows in Q2 likely still elevated but the asymmetric setup has decompressed faster than the 30-day promotion window required.
what_worked: Filing as 'On Our Radar' rather than High Conviction respected the binary geopolitical nature of the catalyst — the lower position size guidance limited drawdown exposure when the war premium deflated.
what_didnt: Thirty-day review window was the right length, but no entry price was recorded so post-mortem return cannot be computed against an actual fill. The May 21 EOG ($139.98) and FANG ($200.97) levels are reference, not entry.
would_have_done: Build a price-trigger ladder into the opp itself: if WTI breaks below $80 before Q2 earnings (late July), reduce sizing or close. The thesis itself flagged $80 as the line — we should have automated the action, not just the watch.

title: Data Center Copper Demand Supercycle
close_reason: Expired — 114-day On Our Radar age-out. Single-name (FCX), no trade construction, and the broader AI-power thesis is now better expressed via ai-power-bottleneck, data-center-grid-buildout, and ai-rack-power-analog-800v. Pattern (Secular Trend) flagged WEAK in ledger (0% win rate over n=4).
what_worked: Identifying the data-center copper-intensity dynamic early — that thesis itself remains directionally correct.
what_didnt: Failed to advance to High Conviction with trade construction. Single ticker, no specific catalyst date, never re-rated within the screener portfolio.
would_have_done: Either commit to a multi-name basket (FCX + SCCO + ERO/IPX) with explicit entry/target/stop in March, or expire faster than 114 days. We let it linger.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock — Gulf Smelters Stranded by Hormuz Closure
close_reason: Expired — catalyst reversing. Iran FM declared the Strait of Hormuz open to all shipping (April 17). US–Iran agreed to a framework to end the three-month conflict with formal signing June 19. LME aluminum has fallen ~11% over the past month to ~$3,258/MT (vs $3,571 four-year high in April). The thesis hinge — 'Hormuz closure stranding Gulf smelter exports' — is unwinding. Radar age-out (42d) forces the decision.
what_worked: Spotting that aluminum's Gulf exposure was less covered than oil — the supply-shock framing was correct while it lasted.
what_didnt: Single-name (AA), no explicit invalidation triggers, no trade construction. Never promoted to High Conviction so we never sized it.
would_have_done: Add explicit price-based invalidations on first filing (e.g., 'aluminum below $3,200 for 14 days') so the close decision is mechanical rather than discretionary.

title: Data Center Copper Demand Supercycle
close_reason: Expired — 115 days on radar without firming up; no specific dated catalyst materialized.
what_worked: Secular thesis (AI datacenter copper intensity) remains directionally correct.
what_didnt: Pattern was 'Secular Trend' which our ledger flags as 0% win rate over 5 prior cases — the bar was too low to surface this with only thematic conviction. Entry/target/stop were never specified, so no objective trigger to act on.
would_have_done: Wait for a firming catalyst — FCX-specific (Grasberg grade ramp surprise, US smelter capex announcement) or sector-wide (copper supply guidance cut from major producer) — before opening. AI-infra exposure is well-covered by other opps (data-center-grid-buildout, ai-power-bottleneck) without copper concentration risk.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock — Gulf Smelters Stranded by Hormuz Closure
close_reason: Duplicate of aluminum-supply-shock (High Conviction, same date_identified, same primary ticker AA). Per de-dup rule: primary ticker AA already lead in another active opp on the same Hormuz-driven supply-shock mechanism. Folded into the High Conviction opp going forward.
what_worked: Thesis and catalyst description are accurate; AA's Q1 windfall and brownfield monetization are real.
what_didnt: Should have been amended into aluminum-supply-shock rather than filed as a separate On Our Radar entry — the de-dup check was missed at the time of opening.
would_have_done: Either fold the Hormuz-specific framing into aluminum-supply-shock as additional context, or differentiate this opp by lifting a non-overlapping ticker (e.g., Rio Tinto, Norsk Hydro) instead of duplicating AA.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock — Gulf Smelters Stranded by Hormuz Closure
close_reason: Duplicate of aluminum-supply-shock (High Conviction, same date_identified, same primary ticker AA). Re-introduced by Step 0 sync from origin/claude/daily-screening-2026-05-13. Already in ledger as duplicate; re-closing.
would_have_done: Skip the Step-0 sync resurrection of any opp whose id already appears as closed in ledger.

title: Data Center Copper Demand Supercycle
close_reason: Expired — 117-day On Our Radar age-out (limit is 30 days). Already closed 3 prior times in ledger for the same age-out; resurrected by Step 0 sync. Single-name (FCX), no firmer trade construction, no specific dated catalyst — fails Step 5 criteria to promote.
what_worked: Identifying secular AI-driven copper demand was correct.
what_didnt: Never tightened catalyst into a dated event; never firmed up entry/target/stop.
would_have_done: If we want exposure to AI copper demand, file a fresh opp with a dated catalyst (e.g., Grasberg permit milestone) and concrete trade construction.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock — Gulf Smelters Stranded by Hormuz Closure
close_reason: Duplicate — same AA / Hormuz aluminum thesis already covered by aluminum-supply-shock (High Conviction, broader AA+CENX). Closing radar duplicate to consolidate.
what_worked: Both opps surfaced the same correct macro setup — Hormuz blockade structurally tightening aluminum supply for North American producers.
what_didnt: Two parallel files for one thesis cluttered the manifest. Should have amended the High Conviction opp rather than open a separate radar variant.
would_have_done: Next time, run de-dup check on theme+primary-ticker before opening a new file.

title: Data Center Copper Demand Supercycle
close_reason: Expired — On Our Radar for 118 days (well past 30-day age-out). Thesis never firmed up with concrete trade construction or fresh primary sources, and the broader 'Secular Trend' pattern has 0/7 win rate in the ledger. Closing per age-out rule; can be re-opened if a specific catalyst emerges.
what_worked: Macro setup (5-10M tonne copper deficit by 2030) remains directionally correct.
what_didnt: No falsifiable 18-month catalyst tied to FCX specifically — pure 'secular trend' framing matches our worst-performing pattern in the ledger.
would_have_done: Either tie to a specific FCX catalyst (Grasberg ramp inflection, smelter expansion completion) or downgrade to Stalking from day one.
